name: San Francisco
metaTitle: E-2 Visa Attorney in San Francisco, CA | Watson Immigration Law
metaDescription: San Francisco attracts E-2 investors from Taiwan, Israel, and across Asia seeking to enter the Bay Area tech ecosystem. Expert E-2 Treaty Investor Visa guidance for San Francisco investors from Watson Immigration Law.
h1: E-2 Treaty Investor Visa Attorney in San Francisco, CA
intro: San Francisco and the broader Bay Area represent the global center of technology innovation, attracting E-2 investors who want to position themselves within the world's most dynamic startup ecosystem. Israeli, Taiwanese, Canadian, and European tech investors are particularly active in the Bay Area E-2 market, typically establishing technology consulting firms, SaaS companies, or startup support businesses that capitalize on proximity to Silicon Valley.

sections.heading: The Bay Area Tech E-2 Market
sections.body: No other E-2 market in the world is as concentrated on technology investment as San Francisco and Silicon Valley. E-2 investors here typically establish businesses that serve the tech ecosystem — IT consulting, software development, cybersecurity, digital marketing, talent acquisition, or technology distribution. Israeli tech investors, Taiwanese semiconductor-adjacent businesses, and Canadian and European tech entrepreneurs are the most active E-2 nationalities in this market.

sections.heading: California Tax Environment
sections.body: California has the highest state income tax in the United States — up to 13.3% — which is a significant consideration for Bay Area E-2 investors. Combined with San Francisco's high cost of living and commercial real estate, the financial model for an E-2 business in San Francisco must project strong revenue to satisfy the non-marginality requirement.

sections.heading: Investment Levels in San Francisco
sections.body: San Francisco's extremely high commercial real estate costs mean that E-2 investments must be proportionally larger. A tech consulting firm with low overhead may qualify with $150,000–$200,000, but any business requiring physical space in San Francisco or the Peninsula will likely require $250,000–$600,000 or more to establish a qualifying enterprise.

stateTax: State income tax up to 13.3% (highest in the U.S.)
uscisOffice: USCIS San Francisco Asylum Office / San Jose Field Office

faqs.question: What type of E-2 business works best in San Francisco?
faqs.answer: Technology-adjacent businesses are by far the most common and most successful E-2 structures in San Francisco. IT consulting, software development, cybersecurity, and startup support services benefit from proximity to Silicon Valley's enormous tech ecosystem. Businesses with relatively low physical space requirements are also more practical given SF's extremely high commercial rents.

faqs.question: What investment level is needed for an E-2 business in San Francisco?
faqs.answer: A technology consulting firm with home-office or co-working space may qualify with $100,000–$200,000. Any business requiring dedicated commercial space in San Francisco will typically require $250,000–$600,000+ due to high rent and build-out costs. The investment must be proportional to the total cost of the enterprise.
